seo.title: Veo 3.1 Lite | AI Video Model, Specs & Pricing | MaxVideoAI
seo.description: Use Veo 3.1 Lite on MaxVideoAI for lower-cost audio-ready drafts, live specs, and pricing before moving selected outputs into Veo 3.1.

overview: Veo 3.1 Lite is the current lower-cost Google Veo 3.1 variant for short text-to-video and image-to-video drafts, timing checks, and prompt testing with native audio included and live pay-per-second pricing.

hero.title: Veo 3.1 Lite — current Veo variant for lower-cost audio-ready drafts
hero.intro: Use Google Veo 3.1 Lite as the lower-cost Veo 3 AI workflow for short text-to-video and image-to-video drafts with native audio always on, then move winning runs into Veo 3.1 Fast or Veo 3.1.

technicalOverview.label: Formats
technicalOverview.body: 16:9 or 9:16; image-based runs can keep auto framing from the source image

technicalOverview.body: Native audio is always on in this tier

promptStructure.title: Text-to-Video with Veo 3.1 Lite
promptStructure.quote: Misty forest at dawn, medium shot centered on a mossy rock. Camera slowly pushes forward as soft rays of light appear between the trees, 6 seconds, 16:9 at 1080p. Audio: gentle birds and wind, no dialogue.
promptStructure.description: Keep prompts tight, single-scene and sensory. Aim to A/B ideas quickly with minimal changes between variants.
steps: Subject + focal point (one idea for the whole clip)
Light and mood (daylight, neon night, warm office, etc.)
Audio cues: ambience + short VO, or say “no dialogue”

tips.title: Tips & limits
items: ⚡ Use Lite as the quickest Veo sandbox for A/B tests and prompt tweaks
🧷 Add a Last frame when the opening and ending frames are already approved
🔊 Audio is always on, so write simple ambience or dialogue cues directly in the prompt
📱 Optimize for social formats where 4–8 seconds is the story
🖼️ Single-scene focus; avoid multi-location narratives in this tier
🔁 Clone winners to Veo 3.1 for final 1080p polish and richer audio

prompts.title: Misty forest slow push
prompts.prompt: Misty forest at dawn, slow push-in toward a mossy rock as light rays cut through trees, subtle birdsong, no dialogue, 8s, 16:9 at 1080p.

prompts.title: Vertical late-night desk loop
prompts.prompt: 6s 9:16 loop of a young woman working late at a minimalist desk, city lights blurred behind her. Slow handheld drift left/right, cool blue window light plus warm desk lamp, quiet typing + city hum, no dialogue.

faqs.q: Is Veo 3.1 Lite good for image-to-video prompt testing?
faqs.a: Yes. Veo 3.1 Lite is a good fit for cheaper image-to-video tests, short audio-ready drafts, and quick prompt checks before moving winners into higher Veo tiers.

faqs.q: When should I use Veo 3.1 Lite instead of Veo 3.1?
faqs.a: Use Veo 3.1 Lite for the cheapest Veo tests, social variants, and internal reviews; switch to Veo 3.1 Fast or Veo 3.1 when you need more control or higher-end finals.

faqs.q: Does Veo 3.1 Lite support vertical videos?
faqs.a: Yes. Veo 3.1 Lite supports 16:9 and 9:16 at 720p or 1080p. Choose 9:16 for mobile-first placements.

faqs.q: Can I reuse Veo 3.1 prompts in Veo 3.1 Lite?
faqs.a: Yes. Clone a Veo 3.1 render, change engine to Veo 3.1 Lite, keep framing/tone presets, and adjust length/resolution.

faqs.q: How does pricing compare to Veo 3.1?
faqs.a: Veo 3.1 Lite is cheaper per second than the other Veo tiers on MaxVideoAI: $0.07/s at 720p or $0.11/s at 1080p, with audio included. Google direct API pricing may differ.

faqs.q: Can I run Veo 3.1 Lite from Europe or LATAM?
faqs.a: Yes. It uses the same MaxVideoAI wallet/workspace and managed provider routing — no separate billing needed.

custom.heroDesc1: Veo 3.1 Lite brings DeepMind’s cinematic engine to a lower price point and faster turnaround, perfect for rapid testing, guided endings, and social loops.
custom.heroDesc2: Start from a short text brief, one start image, or a first/last frame pair, then pick 4/6/8 seconds, choose 16:9 or 9:16, and generate variants fast enough to A/B multiple ideas in one session.
custom.heroSupportLine: Best for exploring hooks, guided endings, and pacing fast before you regenerate winners in Veo 3.1.
heroHighlights: Optimized for speed + business workflows||Built for programmatic ad generation and rapid creative testing at scale.
Keeps Veo’s cinematic intent||Clear camera language + style cues translate well into consistent variants.
Native audio included||Every Lite render ships with audio, which makes quick timing and dialogue tests easier.
Simple upgrade path||Draft in Lite, then regenerate the keepers in Veo 3.1 Fast or Veo 3.1 for higher-fidelity finals.
custom.whatTitle: What Veo 3.1 Lite Actually Is in MaxVideoAI
custom.whatIntro1: Veo 3.1 Lite is tuned for speed and efficiency within the Veo family.
custom.whatIntro2: You keep framing and tone presets, but runs are optimized for cost and turnaround.
whatFlowSteps: 1. Pick Veo 3.1 Lite as the engine.
2. Start from text, add one Start image, or use First/Last frame with one opening and one ending image.
3. Keep reference input focused; Lite is not a multi-style reference stack.
5. Paste a tight, sensory prompt with any simple ambience or dialogue cues.
6. Check the live price and render.

custom.specValueProp: Veo 3.1 Lite is your rapid-fire test bench: same cinematic language as Veo 3.1, lower cost, faster turnaround.
custom.imageTitle: Image and First/Last frame flow with Google Veo 3.1 Lite
custom.imageIntro: Animate one start image, or guide both the opening and ending frame when the landing composition matters.
imageFlow: Upload one lifestyle or product photo as the start image.
Use First/Last frame only when you have both the opening and ending compositions approved.
Prompt subtle camera and subject motion, plus any simple ambience or dialogue cues you need.
Generate variants by changing one element at a time (camera speed, VO tone, time of day).
imageWhy: Quick hero shots for landing pages or ecommerce
Animated social posts from brand photography
Mood loops behind titles or UI elements
custom.multishotIntro1: Use Lite to explore prompts cheaply, then move winners to Veo 3.1 Fast or Veo 3.1.
custom.multishotIntro2: Framing and tone presets are shared, so migration is smooth.
multishotTips: Render 2–4 variants in Lite with small prompt tweaks.
Promote the best to Veo 3.1 Fast or Veo 3.1 for higher fidelity and more workflow control.
Stay single-scene in Lite; use other Veo tiers for multi-beat control.
strengths: Lead with framing + camera move (wide/medium/close + dolly/pan/handheld) before style.
Keep one hero subject and one readable action per clip.
Use 2–3 concrete anchors (time of day, weather, 1–2 set details) instead of long lists.
For continuity, reuse the same “shot recipe” (palette, lighting, lens feel) and change one variable per take.
Audio is best with minimal cues: ambience + 1 key sound, or one short VO line.
boundaries: Single-scene focus works best; multi-location narratives tend to drift.
This tier is draft-first (slightly less detail/fidelity than full Veo 3.1).
Tiny UI text and small lettering are unreliable — add in post.
safetyRules: No sexual content, and nothing involving minors.
No hateful, harassing, or graphic-violence content.
Don’t impersonate real people or public figures; use consent for any likeness/voice.
Don’t include private personal data (addresses, phone numbers, documents, non-consenting faces).
Some prompts or references may be blocked by provider safety filters.
custom.comparisonTitle: Veo 3.1 Lite vs Veo 3.1 – Quick Overview
comparisonPoints: Veo 3.1 Lite: cheaper, faster; best for tests, social loops, internal reviews.
Veo 3.1: higher fidelity and richer audio; best for client-ready and paid media.
Workflow: Prototype in Lite, then regenerate winners in Veo 3.1 Fast or Veo 3.1.

faqs.a: Use Lite to explore ideas, social variants, and internal reviews; use Veo 3.1 Fast or Veo 3.1 for final hero shots and paid media.

custom.relatedSubtitle: Compare price tiers, latency, and prompt presets across the rest of the catalog.
custom.finalPara1: Veo 3.1 Lite in MaxVideoAI is your rapid-fire test bench for Veo prompts.
custom.finalPara2: Same cinematic language, lower stakes, and quality that’s ready for social delivery.
custom.tipsIntro: Veo 3.1 Lite is most reliable when you write a short shot brief: one subject, one action, clear framing, and one camera move.
troubleshootingItems: Drift / ignores details → move subject + action + framing to the first line; remove extra beats; keep constraints positive (“clean background”, “centered subject”).
Motion feels messy → one camera move only; slow the action; simplify the background.
Off-brand look → lock palette + lighting; reuse the same wording; add a reference frame when you can.
Text/signage breaks → keep readable text off-screen; overlay critical copy in post.
VO / lip sync feels off → shorten lines; avoid fast speech and long monologues.

decisionCards.title: Lite or Fast?
decisionCards.body: Use Lite for the lowest-cost Veo drafts. Use Fast when you need reference-to-video control or broader iteration controls.

decisionCards.title: Need audio timing?
decisionCards.body: When audio is enabled, write short ambience, SFX or dialogue cues into the prompt instead of leaving sound vague.

decisionCards.title: Ready for a final pass?
decisionCards.body: Move winning Lite drafts into Veo 3.1 when the concept is approved and needs production polish.

referenceWorkflows.title: Text prompt
referenceWorkflows.body: Use one subject, one action, one camera move and a simple audio cue.

referenceWorkflows.title: Start image
referenceWorkflows.body: Anchor composition or product shape with a single image source.

referenceWorkflows.title: Audio cue
referenceWorkflows.body: Keep ambience or dialogue short when audio is enabled.

referenceWorkflows.title: Short timing test
referenceWorkflows.body: Use 4, 6 or 8 seconds to test the core beat without overbuilding.

referenceWorkflows.title: Upgrade path
referenceWorkflows.body: Promote the best drafts to Fast or Veo 3.1 when you need more control or polish.

section.title: How to Write a Great Veo 3.1 Lite Prompt
section.intro: Veo 3.1 Lite prefers short, concrete prompts and one clear action.
section.tip: Tip: duration + aspect ratio are set in the UI - your prompt controls subject, action, camera, lighting, style, and sound cues.
section.referencesTitle: How Veo 3.1 Lite uses image references

tabs.label: Quick
tabs.title: Quick prompt (fast iteration)
tabs.description: Use 1–2 sentences when you want variations.

tabs.label: Structured
tabs.title: Structured prompt (best for reliable results)
tabs.description: Separate information so the model can follow it consistently.
tabs.copy:
Subject:
[Who/what + one action.]

Context:
[Location + time + 2 visual anchors.]

Camera:
[Shot + angle + one move.]

Look:
[Lighting + palette + style.]

Sound (optional):
[Ambience + 1 cue.]

tabs.label: Pro
tabs.title: Pro prompt (ultra-specific "studio brief")
tabs.description: Use this when you need a very specific look or continuity across shots.
tabs.copy:
Brief:
[Goal + vibe.]

Subject:
[Key traits + wardrobe/props.]

Camera:
[Framing + one move + lens feel.]

Lighting + color:
[Key light + palette anchors.]

Constraints:
Keep it simple; avoid extra actions.

tabs.label: Storyboard
tabs.title: Storyboard prompt (short shot list)
tabs.description: Keep it minimal for faster, cleaner drafts.
tabs.copy:
Shot list (same subject + same setting)
Duration: [4/6/8s]

Shot 1 (0-2s):
[Framing + action + camera move]

Shot 2 (2-6/8s):
[Framing + action + camera move]

Look + mood:
[Lighting + palette]

Sound (optional):
[Ambience + 1 cue]

tabNotes.body: Quick = variations. Use for fast iteration.
Structured = consistency. Use when you need reliable results.
Pro = style control. Use for precise, repeatable looks.

globalPrinciples: Call out framing and camera move.
Use 2-3 visual anchors, not long lists.
engineWhy: Lite favors simple beats and short prompts.
Use style anchors to keep the look stable.

demo.title: Demo: prompt pattern for Veo 3.1 Lite
demo.prompt:
8s Veo 3.1 Lite clip in 16:9, audio included.
Romantic cinematic reunion on a large train station platform, grand period-drama energy, elegant and timeless.
A young couple in early-1900s clothing runs toward each other: the man in a dark wool coat and hat, the woman in a long beige dress with a small vintage suitcase.
Wide crane shot descending slowly toward them, slight long-lens feel, smooth cinematic motion.
Soft morning light filtered through steam and glass, sepia tones, muted browns, brass, and cream. One clear action only, no close foreground signage.
notes: Use one emotional action, not a crowded sequence.
Lock the period details and camera move early.
Keep signage and foreground extras out of the frame.

summary.subject: Romantic train-platform reunion
summary.action: Early-1900s couple runs toward each other
summary.style: Morning light, soft steam, sepia period-drama tones
